leagueBriefing: A rain-softened but still punishing Bay Hill produced a volatile Saturday at the Arnold Palmer Invitational, with Daniel Berger preserving control while the chasing pack tightened before darkness halted play.

games.headline: Berger stays in front at Bay Hill as weather and darkness reshape Round 3
games.brief: Daniel Berger navigated a chaotic third round at Bay Hill to keep the lead in the Arnold Palmer Invitational, reaching 13-under despite seeing his five-shot cushion shrink as conditions shifted after a lengthy rain delay. Akshay Bhatia moved into second at 11-under, while Cameron Young, Sepp Straka and Collin Morikawa posted at 9-under to set up a crowded Sunday chase. Berger's steadiness, plus a key late birdie run before darkness suspended play, kept him in command in a tournament that suddenly felt wide open again.
bullets: Berger held the top spot at 13-under through an unfinished third round, still leading by two.
Bhatia climbed to 11-under and sat closest to Berger entering the overnight restart.
Young shot 67 and joined Straka (66) and Morikawa (70) at 9-under.
Rory McIlroy withdrew before Round 3 because of back muscle spasms.
Scottie Scheffler rallied on the back nine but made double bogey at 18 after finding water.
games.context: With Bay Hill still demanding and several contenders within reach, the delayed finish turned the final round into a multi-player pressure test rather than a runaway.
